phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: At level 1 the numbers lean your way — snare-shield poke combos edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Slight edge at level 2: your snare-shield poke combos beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: At level 3 the numbers lean your way — snare-shield poke combos edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Slight edge through levels 4-5: your snare-shield poke combos beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: At the level-6 breakpoint the numbers lean his way — the wall-frost poke control out-tick your snare-shield poke combos in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Slight deficit on first item: Anivia's wall-frost poke control edge out your snare-shield poke combos on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Respect window: at two-plus items the wall-frost poke control beat your snare-shield poke combos cleanly. Trade only off Anivia's cooldowns and keep the wave where the lead can't cash out.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: E-poke from max range on his last-hits — the slow zone plus passive auto is your chip engine from level 1.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Q joins: the root threat re-prices every step he takes past the wave's midline.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Full kit: Q-E-auto with the passive detonations is a real chunk. Shield his answer and walk.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Through levels 4-5 your snare-shield poke combos beat the stardust ramp control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Aurelion Sol answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Your snare-shield poke combos out-muscle Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control at the level-6 breakpoint — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: On first item the numbers lean your way — snare-shield poke combos edge out the stardust ramp control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Lux decides fights from a screen away: root the entry, slow the pit, and laser the clump your team froze.

phases.why: At level 1 the numbers lean his way — the reflect-barrier poke out-tick your snare-shield poke combos in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Slight deficit at level 2: Mel's reflect-barrier poke edge out your snare-shield poke combos on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: At level 3 the numbers lean his way — the reflect-barrier poke out-tick your snare-shield poke combos in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Slight deficit through levels 4-5: Mel's reflect-barrier poke edge out your snare-shield poke combos on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: At the level-6 breakpoint the numbers lean his way — the reflect-barrier poke out-tick your snare-shield poke combos in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Slight deficit on first item: Mel's reflect-barrier poke edge out your snare-shield poke combos on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: At two-plus items the numbers lean his way — the reflect-barrier poke out-tick your snare-shield poke combos in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: At level 1 the numbers lean your way — snare-shield poke combos edge out the clone-root deception when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Slight edge at level 2: your snare-shield poke combos beat Neeko's clone-root deception on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Full kit: Q-E-auto with the passive detonations is a real chunk. Shield his answer and walk.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Slight edge through levels 4-5: your snare-shield poke combos beat Neeko's clone-root deception on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Final Spark online — anything the root catches dies to the laser math. The lane becomes a geometry quiz.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Luden's spike: Q-E-R deletes squishies from root range. Force him to dodge or die on every contact.
